opportunities.title: CISA's Worst Week: Fresh SharePoint RCE Zero-Day (CVE-2026-58644) Added to KEV Within Days of Disclosure
opportunities.why_now: CISA just added a newly disclosed SharePoint RCE to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities list mere days after the flaw surfaced, on the heels of an already-flagged trio of SharePoint bugs.
opportunities.angle: Timeline breakdown: disclosure-to-exploitation clock, patch status table, and why SharePoint keeps being the softest target in the Microsoft stack

opportunities.title: 570 Flaws, One Tuesday: Inside Microsoft's Record-Breaking Patch Drop
opportunities.why_now: Krebs reports Microsoft just shipped a record 570 security fixes in a single Patch Tuesday, raising real triage-capacity alarms for defenders.
opportunities.angle: Patch-priority digest: which of the 570 are actually exploited/critical, plus a snark aside on 'patch cadence' becoming 'patch avalanche'

opportunities.title: Ransomware Just Stopped Fairlife: Coca-Cola's US Production Line Goes Dark
opportunities.why_now: Coca-Cola confirmed it suspended US Fairlife production due to a ransomware attack, a rare admission of physical operational impact from a Fortune 500 brand.
opportunities.angle: Breach-to-shelf-impact narrative: what ransomware disruption looks like when it hits a beverage supply chain, tied into the OT/legacy-systems piece from the same week

opportunities.title: Qantas Got Social-Engineered, Not Hacked: The Tech Support Scam That Blew Open an Airline's Data
opportunities.why_now: Qantas's massive data breach traces back to a tech support scam rather than a technical exploit, reinforcing the 'identity attacks over exploits' trend flagged this same week.
opportunities.angle: Root-cause postmortem pairing the Qantas incident with Dark Reading's 'Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause' data point — the human perimeter is the new CVE

opportunities.title: 'Just Paste This Into Terminal': The ClickFix Con Is Now an Industry
opportunities.why_now: A macOS 'paste this fix into Terminal' scam and a new ACR Stealer campaign using ClickFix lures both surfaced this week, showing the same social-engineering technique scaling across platforms and malware families.
opportunities.angle: Pattern-recognition piece cataloguing every ClickFix-style lure seen this week (macOS Terminal scam + ACR Stealer) as a single emerging attacker playbook, with a 'never paste this' reader checklist
